evals.prompt: I need to use jj to commit my changes
evals.expected_output: Should trigger jujutsu skill based on jj mention

assertions.name: triggers-jujutsu
assertions.description: Triggers the jujutsu skill

evals.prompt: how do I create a bookmark in jujutsu?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger jujutsu skill based on jujutsu mention

assertions.name: triggers-jujutsu
assertions.description: Triggers the jujutsu skill

evals.prompt: help me set up jj in this repo
evals.expected_output: Should trigger jujutsu skill; may also suggest /jj-init command

assertions.name: triggers-jujutsu
assertions.description: Triggers the jujutsu skill

evals.prompt: what's the jj equivalent of git cherry-pick?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger jujutsu skill and reference rebase

assertions.name: triggers-jujutsu
assertions.description: Triggers the jujutsu skill

evals.prompt: commit this with a message
evals.context: No .jj/ directory exists in the repository root
evals.expected_output: Generic commit request should NOT trigger jujutsu unless .jj/ is present

assertions.name: does-not-trigger-jujutsu
assertions.description: Does not trigger jujutsu skill for generic commit

evals.prompt: check my repo status
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory
evals.expected_output: Should trigger jujutsu skill when .jj/ directory exists even without explicit jj mention

assertions.name: triggers-jujutsu-via-dotjj
assertions.description: Triggers jujutsu skill from .jj/ directory presence

evals.prompt: run /jj-init to set up jujutsu in this repo
evals.expected_output: /jj-init is a slash command, not a skill — should invoke directly

assertions.name: invokes-command
assertions.description: Invokes the /jj-init slash command

evals.prompt: help me resolve this git merge conflict
evals.context: Repo has .git/ directory but no .jj/ directory
evals.expected_output: Git-specific merge conflict with no .jj/ should NOT trigger jujutsu skill

assertions.name: does-not-trigger-jujutsu-for-git
assertions.description: Does not trigger jujutsu skill for git-only workflow

evals.prompt: check my repo status
evals.context: Repo root contains both .jj/ and .git/ directories (colocated jj+git repo)
evals.expected_output: Should trigger jujutsu skill in colocated repo where both .jj/ and .git/ exist

assertions.name: triggers-jujutsu-colocated
assertions.description: Triggers jujutsu skill in colocated jj+git repo

evals.prompt: check my repo status
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory
evals.expected_output: Should use jj st instead of git status. Should detect .jj/ and activate jujutsu skill.

assertions.name: uses-jj-status
assertions.description: Uses jj st for status check

assertions.name: does-not-use-git-status
assertions.description: Does not fall back to git status

evals.prompt: commit these changes with message 'fix: update config'
evals.context: Active repo is a jj repo
evals.expected_output: Should use jj commit -m, not jj desc + jj new, and not git add + git commit

assertions.name: uses-jj-commit
assertions.description: Uses jj commit -m for committing

assertions.name: no-staging
assertions.description: Does not use git add or jj track for regular files

evals.prompt: push my changes to the remote
evals.context: Active jj repo has bookmark 'feature-x'
evals.expected_output: Should use jj git push -b feature-x, not git push

assertions.name: uses-jj-git-push
assertions.description: Uses jj git push with bookmark flag

assertions.name: specifies-bookmark
assertions.description: Includes -b or --bookmark flag

evals.prompt: split this commit into two
evals.context: Active repo is a jj repo
evals.expected_output: Should refuse or warn that jj split is interactive and cannot be used by agents

assertions.name: warns-interactive
assertions.description: Warns that split is interactive
assertions.check: Output mentions that jj split is interactive and not agent-safe

assertions.name: warns-interactive-auto
assertions.description: Automated check for interactive warning

evals.prompt: run git rebase in this repo
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory
evals.expected_output: Should warn against using mutating git commands in colocated jj repo

assertions.name: warns-no-git-mutate
assertions.description: Warns against git mutating commands in jj repos
assertions.check: Output warns that mutating git commands should not be used in jj repos

assertions.name: warns-no-git-mutate-auto
assertions.description: Automated check for git mutation warning

evals.prompt: run git log to see recent commits in this jj repo
evals.context: Repo root contains both .jj/ and .git/ directories (colocated repo)
evals.expected_output: Should NOT warn against read-only git commands like git log in a colocated jj repo. May suggest jj log as alternative but should not block git log.

assertions.name: no-warning-for-git-log
assertions.description: Does not warn against or block read-only git commands in colocated repo
assertions.check: Verify no warning about using git log in jj repo; may suggest jj log as alternative but does not refuse

evals.prompt: create a jj workspace with the same name as an existing one
evals.context: Active repo is a jj repo
evals.expected_output: Should detect the naming collision and either use a unique name or report the conflict

assertions.name: handles-collision
assertions.description: Handles workspace name collision gracefully
assertions.check: Verify the agent detects or handles workspace name collision

evals.prompt: create a worktree for feature work named 'my-feature'
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory. Worktree hooks are configured.
evals.expected_output: Should use worktree-{NAME} prefix format for workspace names (e.g., worktree-my-feature)

assertions.name: uses-worktree-prefix
assertions.description: Workspace name uses worktree- prefix convention

evals.prompt: initialize jj in this repo
evals.context: Repo already has .jj/ directory
evals.expected_output: Should detect existing .jj/ and inform user that jj is already initialized

assertions.name: detects-existing
assertions.description: Detects existing jj initialization

evals.prompt: I'm in a jj repo and need to review a PR — what VCS commands should agents use?
evals.expected_output: Should reference VCS detection preamble and jj commands for agents

assertions.name: references-vcs-detection
assertions.description: References the VCS detection preamble command

evals.prompt: check repo status
evals.context: Directory has no .jj/ or .git/ present
evals.expected_output: Should detect no VCS and report appropriately

assertions.name: detects-no-vcs
assertions.description: Reports no VCS detected when neither .jj/ nor .git/ exists
assertions.check: Verify agent reports no VCS or asks user to initialize

evals.prompt: I need to push my current changes but I haven't set a bookmark. How do I push in jj?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend jj git push --change or creating a bookmark first
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory. User has uncommitted changes with no bookmark set.

assertions.name: suggests-push-change
assertions.description: Suggests jj git push --change or bookmark creation

evals.prompt: /jj-init
evals.context: Repo has .jj/ directory already present
evals.expected_output: Should detect existing .jj/ and stop with 'already initialized' message

assertions.name: detects-already-initialized
assertions.description: Reports that jj is already initialized

evals.prompt: /jj-init
evals.context: Repo has .git/ directory but no .jj/ directory
evals.expected_output: Should run jj git init --colocate, update .gitignore, verify with jj st

assertions.name: runs-jj-git-init-colocate
assertions.description: Runs jj git init --colocate

assertions.name: updates-gitignore
assertions.description: Updates .gitignore for jj

assertions.name: runs-verification
assertions.description: Runs jj st to verify initialization succeeded

evals.prompt: /jj-init
evals.context: Directory has no .git/ or .jj/ present
evals.expected_output: Should detect no git repo and report error, suggest git init first

assertions.name: suggests-git-init
assertions.description: Suggests running git init first

evals.prompt: Initialize jj in this repo
evals.context: jj git init --colocate exits non-zero with error: 'Error: The Git repo is already colocated'
evals.expected_output: Should report the error text to the user and stop without modifying .gitignore or running verification

assertions.name: surfaces-error-text
assertions.description: Surfaces the jj init error text to the user

assertions.name: reports-failure
assertions.description: Reports that init failed rather than silently continuing

assertions.name: no-gitignore-shell-command
assertions.description: Does not execute the step 4 gitignore modification shell command

evals.prompt: git commit -m 'fix: update readme'
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger jujutsu skill for explicit git commands in a git-only repo

assertions.name: no-jujutsu-trigger
assertions.description: Does not trigger jujutsu skill for explicit git commands

evals.prompt: /jj-init
evals.context: jj binary is not installed (command -v jj returns non-zero)
evals.expected_output: Should detect missing jj, tell user to install it with URL, and stop without running any further steps

assertions.name: surfaces-install-url
assertions.description: References jj install docs or suggests installation

assertions.name: stops-early
assertions.description: Does not proceed to git rev-parse or jj git init

evals.prompt: jj bookmark set failed, should I run jj undo twice to clean up?
evals.context: In a jj repo, jj bookmark set was attempted but failed with an error
evals.expected_output: Should explain that failed jj commands leave no op-log entry, so jj undo only needs to run once (or not at all for the failed operation)

assertions.name: no-undo-twice
assertions.description: Does not recommend running jj undo twice

assertions.name: explains-op-log
assertions.description: Explains that failed operations leave no op-log entry

evals.prompt: the worktree creation just failed with an ERROR about leaked workspace metadata
evals.context: worktree-create.sh returned non-zero with stderr containing: ERROR: cleanup: jj workspace forget worktree-my-feature failed — workspace metadata may be leaked
evals.expected_output: Should surface the error and instruct the user to run 'jj workspace forget worktree-my-feature' manually to recover

assertions.name: tells-user-forget-command
assertions.description: Agent tells user to run jj workspace forget

assertions.name: indicates-manual-recovery
assertions.description: Agent indicates that manual recovery is needed
